ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, INTERVENORS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO LIMIT THE SCOPE OF PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF DEPOSITION OF INTERVENORS'S PERSON MOST QUALIFIED. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. THE COURT ALSO SIGNS THE PARTIES' STIPULATION AND PROPOSED ORDER RE: DEPOSITION OF CUSTODIAN OF RECORD AND PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE THAT WAS PRESENTED ON THE EX PARTE CALENDAR LAST WEEK. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Limit The Scope Of Plaintiffs Notice Of Deposition Of Intervenorss Person Most Qualified
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: INTERVENORS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO LIMIT THE SCOPE OF PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF DEPOSITION OF INTERVENORS'S PERSON MOST QUALIFIED.

Appearances: Gardiner McKleroy of Wilson Elser (415.625.9268) for intervenors and moving parties; Gilbert Purcell of Brayton Purcell LLP (415.898.1555) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. The scope of Intervenors' request for protective order is overly broad. Intervenors have not shown that a protective order is needed to prevent "unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense." (Code Civ. Proc. section 2025.420.) Plaintiffs are entitled to take the deposition of Intervenors' PMQ. (see Boskowitz v. Thompson (1904) 144 Cal. 724, 728, 78 ["Upon being permitted to intervene, the intervenor is to be regarded as a plaintiff or as a defendant in the action (unless he seeks something adversely to both) according as is the party for whose success he seeks to intervene, and is limited to the same procedure and remedies as is such original party, either for the purpose of defeating the action or resisting the claim of the plaintiff"].) Discovery in a deposition is not limited to a deponent's "personal knowledge." Further, if a witness lacks knowledge, the witness may indicate as such. Intervenors' argument that any information sought by Plaintiffs as to whether Intervenors insured D/C Distribution will be inadmissible at trial is not grounds for a protective order. (Code Civ. Proc. section 2017.010; Lipton v. Superior Ct. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 1599, 1611-12 ["Admissibility is not the test and information, unless privileged, is discoverable if it might reasonably lead to admissible evidence"], citing Davies v. Superior Court (1984) 36 Cal.3d 291, 301.) Order signed. The Court also signs the parties' stipulation and proposed order re: deposition of custodian of record and person most knowledgeable that was presented on the ex parte calendar last week. Judge: Cynthia M. Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR 13859 (T: 714.471.2597, E: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com) (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Maintaining The Current Trial Date With Preference
Off Calendar Mar-10-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff via CourtCall.
Gayle Ketchie of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant D/C DISTRIBUTION CORPORATION.

RULING:
Contested as to a portion of the tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified:
Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Maintaining the Current Trial Date with Preference is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). Intervenors Ace Property and Casualty Company, et al. filed an opposition.

The current trial date of May 16, 2022 is vacated. The new trial date is June 27, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 8, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is June 10, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 24, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than, April 8, 2022. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

No later than March 24, 2022, plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar.
